title: Poecilostachys geminata
intro: Poecilostachys geminata, also known as Indian gentian or gentianella, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce stress and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Poecilostachys geminata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, extracts, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and anxiety disorders.
taxonomy: Poecilostachys geminata is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification places it in the kingdom Plantae. More specifically, it belongs to the order Poales, which includes other grasses and sedges. Poecilostachys geminata is further classified into the family Poaceae, subfamily Bambusoideae, and tribe Arundinarieae. It is a member of the monocotyledonous plants, which have one cotyledon in their seeds.

properties: Poecilostachys geminata is also known as Indian spikenard or valerian of the mountains.
